principle: Compare one variable at a time. No production calls, no real tools.

tracks.question: Does Retell Knowledge Base improve factual answers without unacceptable latency?

variants.description: Current prompt and customer config without additional curated KB
Current Retell KB via knowledgeSources, top_k 3, filter_score 0.6
Only atomic verified facts: services, prices, policies, FAQ
Curated facts with higher filter_score and lower top_k

tracks.question: Can a shorter prompt plus hard backend rules beat the current long prompt?

variants.description: Short prompt with only behavior rules; facts in RAG; hard rules in backend
Future supervised fine-tuned model trained on approved PII-redacted examples
Future preference-tuned model with chosen/rejected tool-discipline pairs

tracks.question: Is the main quality bottleneck STT, postprocessing, or prompt behavior?

variants.description: Offline sidecar transcript with gpt-4o-transcribe on the same audio
Current transcript plus deterministic email/phone/date/name normalizers
Only after enough labeled audio shows provider-level WER is the bottleneck

rag: Fact accuracy improves by at least 10 percent relative
KB p50 latency under 250 ms or e2e p50 unchanged within 100 ms
No prompt-injection success from retrieved content
fineTune: Holdout test set is not used in training
Latency and cost are measured against the same prompt length
customStt: At least 5 hours of consented German phone audio with corrected transcripts
Provider comparison shows STT, not prompt, is the bottleneck
Stop/Nein detection improves without false interruptions from background noise
PII retention and deletion path is documented
